2606.02301.title: Quantitative Movement Testing: Measuring Patient Movements from a Single Smartphone Video
2606.02301.paper_summary: Chronic pain diminishes quality of life by decreasing functional ability, yet objectively measuring this functional impact remains challenging in real-world settings. While optical motion capture provides high precision for assessing altered movement quality, it is costly and restricted to laboratory environments. We aimed to develop and validate Quantitative Movement Testing (QMT), a computer vision pipeline extracting 3D kinematic biomarkers from standard monocular smartphone video, balancing clinical accessibility with biomechanical accuracy. We validated the QMT pipeline, utilising deep learning-based 3D pose-estimation, against gold-standard optical motion capture in healthy controls (N=13). Following leave-one-subject-out calibration to correct systematic bias, we deployed QMT in two prospective clinical cohorts to assess real-world utility: a pre- and post-intervention trial for fibromyalgia patients, and a 30-day longitudinal at-home monitoring study of chronic sciatica patients and healthy controls. In laboratory validation, QMT extracted clinical kinematic metrics with high agreement to optical motion capture, yielding strong correlations (r > 0.85) and low mean absolute errors. QMT demonstrated high test-retest reliability (r > 0.86) in fibromyalgia patients and successfully tracked day-to-day movement fluctuations in chronic sciatica. While real-world home settings introduced higher measurement variance than lab settings, QMT found group-level differences between healthy controls and sciatica patients based entirely on remote recordings. Monocular 3D pose estimation offers a scalable alternative to traditional assessments. QMT provides an objective, accessible biomarker for tracking disease progression and treatment response in clinical trials, though further research is needed to optimise reliability in home environments.
2606.02301.paper_summary_zh: 慢性疼痛透過降低功能能力來減少生活品質，但在現實世界中客觀測量這種功能影響仍然具有挑戰性。雖然光學運動捕捉提供了高精度來評估改變的運動質量，但其成本高且僅限於實驗室環境。我們的目標是開發和驗證定量運動測試（QMT），這是一個從標準單眼智能手機視頻中提取3D運動生物標記的計算機視覺管道，平衡臨床可及性與生物力學準確性。我們驗證了QMT管道，利用基於深度學習的3D姿勢估計，與健康對照組的金標準光學運動捕捉進行比較（N=13）。在進行留一個受試者校準以修正系統性偏差後，我們在兩個前瞻性臨床隊列中部署了QMT，以評估其在現實世界中的實用性：一項針對纖維肌痛患者的干預前後試驗，以及一項針對慢性坐骨神經痛患者和健康對照的30天長期居家監測研究。在實驗室驗證中，QMT提取的臨床運動學指標與光學運動捕捉高度一致，產生了強相關性（r > 0.85）和低平均絕對誤差。QMT在纖維肌痛患者中顯示出高的測試-重測信度（r > 0.86），並成功追蹤了慢性坐骨神經痛患者的日常運動波動。雖然現實世界的居家環境引入了比實驗室環境更高的測量變異，但QMT在完全基於遠程錄音的情況下發現了健康對照組和坐骨神經痛患者之間的群體差異。單眼3D姿勢估計為傳統評估提供了一種可擴展的替代方案。QMT提供了一種客觀、可及的生物標記，用於追蹤臨床試驗中的疾病進展和治療反應，但仍需進一步研究以優化居家環境中的可靠性。
2606.02301.author: Pranav Mahajan et.al.
2606.02301.authors: Pranav Mahajan, Amanda Wall, Eleonora Maria Camerone, Julie Stebbins, Eoin Kelleher, Shuangyi Tong, Annina Schmid, Katja Wiech, Anushka Irani, Ben Seymour
